output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? The 60-Second Demand Letter That Gets Results
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your deposit? Send a legal demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99. No signup or subscription needed. Learn your tenant rights now.
content_outline: How to Know If Your Landlord Is Illegally Withholding Your Deposit
State-by-State Deadlines: When Your Security Deposit Is Legally Due
5 Things a Landlord Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
Why a Demand Letter Works Better Than Small Claims Court (For Now)
How to Generate Your Official Demand Letter in 60 Seconds for $19.99
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days; missing this deadline may entitle tenants to penalties or double damages.
Normal wear and tear—such as faded paint, minor carpet wear, and loose door handles—cannot be legally deducted from a tenant's deposit.
A formal letter of demand is often the fastest, most effective way to force compliance before filing in small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com lets U.S. renters generate a legally formatted security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord hold your money hostage. Visit Deposits-Back.com and generate your official security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds. Flat $19.99. No signup. No subscription. Get your deposit back today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters who have moved out and are dealing with a landlord who is wrongfully withholding, deducting from, or delaying the return of their security deposit.
